reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: An attacker can trigger this via P2P gossip by acting as a malicious proposer that creates two different blocks at the same slot with different bodyRoots, producing valid data column sidecars for both. The deduplication logic uses blockRootHex (which differs for the two blocks) instead of the spec-required (slot, proposer_index, index) tuple, allowing both sidecars to be accepted and cached separately. This violates the PeerDAS specification's invariant that only the first sidecar for a given (slot, proposer_index, column_index) should be accepted.
reviewed_items.spec_reference: EIP-7594 PeerDAS - deduplication requirement for data column sidecars
